Class CustomTreeWalker

java.lang.Object
recoder.convenience.AbstractTreeWalker
recoder.convenience.TreeWalker
recoder.convenience.CustomTreeWalker
All Implemented Interfaces:
Cloneable, ProgramElementWalker
public class CustomTreeWalker extends TreeWalker
A tree walker that allows to customize the traversal. The walker can report elements during ascending, and may skip the traversal of certain subtrees on demand.

  • Method Details

    • isReportingReturns

      public boolean isReportingReturns()
      Checks whether non terminal elements are reported when returning from recursion. Default is false (no report).

    • setReportingReturns

      public void setReportingReturns(boolean yes)
      Decide whether non terminal elements should be reported when returning from recursion.

    • isReturning

      public boolean isReturning()
      Returns true if the current element is left. This element will not be reported any longer. If return reporting is switched off, only terminal elements will be reported as returning.
      See Also:
      • isReportingReturns

    • setParentFilter

      public void setParentFilter(ModelElementFilter filter)
      Set a filter that decides whether the children of a non terminal should be reported, or not. This setting takes effect beginning at the next call to next(). It does not affect reports of returning nodes.

    • setChildFilter

      public void setChildFilter(ModelElementFilter filter)

    • next

      public boolean next()
      Description copied from class: AbstractTreeWalker
      Proceeds to the next element, if available. Returns true, if there is one, false otherwise.
      Specified by:
      next in interface ProgramElementWalker
      Overrides:
      next in class TreeWalker
      Returns:
      true if the iterator points to an object.
